title: Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur
tagline: Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Virateshwar temple or Virat temple of Lord Shiva is located in Sohagpur (Shahdol), Shahdol district of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: Virateshwar temple or Virat temple of Lord Shiva is located in Sohagpur (Shahdol), Shahdol district of Madhya Pradesh. The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) Monument number for this temple is N-MP-255. ASI database mentions it as "Virath Temple and remains". This temple was built by the Kalchuri ruler, Maharaja Yuvarajadeva II between 950 CE and 1050 CE. This was built as a present for the Acharya (saint) of Golkaki Math.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jabalpur Airport (134 km) and the nearest railway station is Katni Junction (114 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur?
faq.a: Stays in Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Virateshwar temple, Sohagpur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shahdol division, Shahdol, Sohagpur (Shahdol).
